evals.prompt: I have a pile of println-based CLI output showing grep hits and semantic references. Refactor it into a Kotter screen with a boxed act header, a side-by-side comparison grid, and a bounded summary note when rows are truncated. Keep the renderer testable.
evals.expected_output: A small display model plus a pure RenderScope renderer using bordered/grid primitives, with explicit truncation messaging and tests that assert rendered content through Kotter test support.

evals.prompt: Build a Kotter interactive selector for a two-step demo: Act 1 is a static summary, Act 2 is a file viewport that uses arrow keys to move between files and semantic hits. I want the navigation logic to be easy to unit test.
evals.expected_output: A pure state machine for navigation, a focus/view computation for the current viewport, and a session-driven Kotter UI that updates via onKeyPressed without mixing rendering and state transitions.

evals.prompt: Show me how to test a Kotter widget that handles typed input, Enter, and ESC. I care about in-memory terminal tests more than end-to-end shell scripts.
evals.expected_output: A testSession-based approach using terminal.type, terminal.press, stripFormatting or blockUntilRenderMatches as appropriate, plus explanation of when to use sendKeys versus literal terminal typing.

evals.prompt: Refactor this raw terminal status output into a styled Kotter dashboard. I want semantic colors for info/warn/error lines, a bordered summary panel, and a small table for current tasks. Keep the styling reusable instead of hardcoding colors everywhere.
evals.expected_output: A Kotter-based screen that uses small semantic RenderScope wrappers for styling, bordered/grid layout helpers for structure, and a display-model-first approach rather than inline formatting logic.

evals.prompt: Build a polished terminal splash / progress experience in Kotter: centered-ish title, one-shot intro animation, then a live status panel that appends finished steps into history while a spinner keeps updating below.
evals.expected_output: A Kotter design using shiftRight or justified plus render/text animations for the intro, then section plus aside for active-vs-history output, with timers/live state instead of manual ANSI control.
